channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New H5N9 bird flu strain found in California, first case in the US
item.description: The first confirmed case of H5N9 bird flu in the United States has been identified in Merced County, California, and control measures are being undertaken to contain the virus.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, KREX 5

item.title: Egg prices are soaring. Don't expect that to change anytime soon
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Bird flu is forcing farmers to slaughter millions of chickens a month, pushing U.S. egg prices to more than double their cost in the summer of 2023. And it appears there may be no relief in sight, given the the surge in demand as Easter approaches.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, MARK VANCLEAVE and DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Beneficiaries under Social Security Fairness Act could be waiting 'more than one year' for adjusted payments, SSA says
item.description: The Social Security Administration, in an update to its website on Friday, said the department does not currently have the funding to execute the adjustments “in a timely manner and without negatively affecting day-to-day customer service.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Houston-area school districts address immigration enforcement policies amid federal crackdowns
item.description: In response to President Trump's heightened immigration policies, Houston-area schools and law enforcement are clarifying their protocols regarding federal agency requests, prioritizing student safety.
item.dc:creator: Rilwan Balogun, Holly Galvan Posey, KPRC 2

item.title: Live updates: Trump addresses House Republicans at retreat
item.description: President Trump is set to address the House Republicans at their annual policy retreat in Florida, while the Senate votes on the confirmation of his Treasury Department nominee, Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Earthquake off coast of Maine shakes Northeast
item.description: A magnitude 3.8 earthquake off the coast of Maine shook parts of the Northeast on Monday morning, with residents as far away as Boston feeling the rumble, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, Fox 5

item.title: Is cell service getting worse? One study suggests that it is
item.description: The study, from J.D. Power, showed an increase in reported wireless network problems between the first and the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration carries out multiple raids targeting ‘criminal aliens’ in first weekend
item.description: ICE agents and their partners in federal and local law enforcement ramped up targeted raids over the weekend, seeking known "criminal aliens" accused of U.S. crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Connecticut residents report feeling Maine earthquake
item.description: Some people in Connecticut have reported feeling an earthquake recorded off the coast of Maine Monday morning, according to data from the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's move to freeze Biden-approved funding draws howls from Democrats
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over President Trump's move to freeze funding approved in two of former President Biden's signature laws. Trump issued an executive order pausing the disbursement of funds appropriated through the Inflation Reduction Act and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Repeat sex trafficker in Mass. sentenced to 13 years in prison
item.description: A Stoughton, Massachusetts man who has prior convictions of sex trafficking has been sentenced to 13 years in prison for sex trafficking multiple adult women.
item.dc:creator: Beck Wehner, NewsNation

item.title: Haitian migrants in Springfield, Ohio, turn to faith amid deportation fears
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ohio (AP) — At the end of his Sunday service, the pastor of the First Haitian Evangelical Church of Springfield, Ohio, asked ushers and musicians to form a circle around him as he knelt in prayer, flanked by the flags of Haiti and the United States.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO and JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ryan Pressly waives no-trade clause to join the Chicago Cubs, AP source says
item.description: The Chicago Cubs are finalizing a trade for Ryan Pressly, adding the right-hander to the back end of their bullpen in their second major offseason deal with the Houston Astros.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Qatar says a deal is reached to release an Israeli hostage and allow Palestinians into northern Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Qatar announced early Monday that an agreement has been reached to release an Israeli civilian hostage and allow Palestinians to return to northern Gaza, easing the first major crisis of the fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Researchers studying whether medical cannabis could help late-stage dementia patients
item.description: As research continues, those involved say this would be a game changer, especially for patients with very limited options.
item.dc:creator: Josh Shortt, ABC 4

item.title: What is a migrant? What is ICE? 10 terms to help you understand the debate over immigration
item.description: Understanding the many different immigration terms – some technical, some not – can help people better understand immigration news.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Castañeda Professor of Latin American and Latino studies, American University, ABC 4

item.title: Trump cuts Dr. Fauci's security detail
item.description: Asked if he would feel partially responsible if something were to happen to Fauci, Trump said he would not.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: North Dakota’s overturned abortion ban won’t be in effect during appeal, court rules
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota’s abortion ban will not be enforced while the state appeals an earlier decision that found it unconstitutional, the state's highest court ruled Friday. That appeal has yet to fully play out in the state Supreme Court after a judge struck down the law in September.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Trump's first executive actions on climate and environment
item.description: President Donald Trump’s first week in office included a flurry of executive orders with implications for Earth’s climate and environment.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: State lawmakers are pushing for vaccine exemptions even as childhood vaccination rates fall
item.description: Vaccination bills are popping up in more than 15 states as lawmakers aim to potentially resurrect or create new religious exemptions from immunization mandates, establish state-level vaccine injury databases or dictate what providers must tell patients about the shots.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh And Devi Shastri, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: SZA to join Kendrick Lamar for Super Bowl LIX halftime show
item.description: Apple Music released a teaser trailer on Thursday showing the Compton native on the gridiron, assessing the field before the big game while he's talking to someone on the phone.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Marilyn Manson won't face domestic violence and sexual assault charges, DA says
item.description: "We recognize and applaud the courage and resilience of the women who came forward to make reports and share their experiences, and we thank them for their cooperation and patience with the investigation," the district attorney's office said in a news release explaining the decision not to pursue ch
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Homeowners in Palisades Fire area angered by bank's decision
item.description: With the devastating Palisades Fire still smoldering in California, Lisa Pelton and some of her neighbors in Mandeville Canyon received an unpleasant notice from their bank: Their home equity lines of credit were being slashed.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders release of JFK, RFK and MLK assassination records
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of classified governmental documents about the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy, which has fueled conspiracy theories for decades.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mississippi state lawmaker introduces 'Contraception Begins at Erection Act' with fines for masturbation
item.description: A Democratic Mississippi state senator introduced legislation this week that makes it unlawful for men to masturbate "without the intent to fertilize an embryo," with the lawmaker criticizing anti-abortion measures that only "focus on the woman’s role.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump targets California water policy as he prepares to tour LA fire damage
item.description: As President Donald Trump prepares to tour wildfire damage in California, he's zeroing in on one of his frequent targets for criticism: State water policy. Since the fires broke out Jan.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Progress is made on a huge fire north of Los Angeles while new fires erupt in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Evacuation orders were lifted Thursday for tens of thousands as firefighters with air support slowed the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but new blazes erupted in San Diego County, briefly triggering more evacuations.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Over 50,000 under evacuation orders or warnings as wildfire imperils homes north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — More than 50,000 people were under evacuation orders or warnings Wednesday as a huge and fast-moving wildfire swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but fire officials said a rapid ground and air assault was giving them the upper hand.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
